title: Ukraine used 10 AI-controlled Terminator drones to kill Russian soldiers two years ago, marking first autonomous killings of humans autonomous killer quadcopters left everything dead says senior Ukrainian defense industry figure
summary: In 2024, Ukraine deployed 10 AI-controlled quadcopters to kill Russian soldiers.
insight: Autonomous AI systems are now capable of making lethal decisions.

title: Dev releases unblockable ASCII video stream software delivers 360p video at 30 FPS and acts as a bridge for AI
summary: A new software delivers high-performance, real-time ASCII video rendering, capable of broadcasting 360p video at 30 FPS, offering an unblockable video stream solution. It acts as a bridge for AI-powered applications, providing a unique video streaming solution. This software promises to revolutionize the way we stream video content.
insight: Unblockable video stream bypasses traditional content delivery network restrictions

title: Thrustmaster's new specialized T.Flight Hotas 5 Microsoft Flight Simulator Edition provides a plug-and-play flight sim setup for just $109 featuring 5-axis control with 16-bit precision and dual-rudder system
summary: The T.Flight Hotas 5 provides a plug-and-play flight sim setup with 5-axis control and 16-bit precision, suitable for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024 and compatible with both PC and PlayStation consoles.
insight: Accurate 16-bit precision enhances the realism of flight simulation.

title: Radeon RX 9070 XT finally appears in Steam Hardware Survey RDNA 4 flagship surprisingly lands just behind RTX 5080
summary: AMD's Radeon RX 9070 XT has entered the Steam Hardware Survey, ranking 25th. This marks a milestone in the card's adoption rate, indicating growing market presence. Its positioning behind the NVIDIA RTX 5080 suggests a competitive landscape.
insight: Radeon RX 9070 XT's Steam entry highlights AMD's GPU market traction

title: Nvidia's high-speed AI data center storage servers break cover, touting 2.9 petabytes of storage and extreme PCIe 6.0 performance Wiwynn shows off SCADA server with GPU-accelerated storage
summary: Wiwynn showcases Nvidia's high-speed AI data center storage servers with 2.9 petabytes of storage and extreme PCIe 6.0 performance. The server features GPU-accelerated storage acceleration for AI systems. This technology promises ultra-fast storage and improved AI processing capabilities.
insight: GPU-accelerated storage boosts AI system performance and efficiency significantly.

title: Microsoft's bug-hunting nemesis extends vendetta with more zero-day attacks Nightmare Eclipse publishes RoguePlanet and GreatXML local privilege escalation exploits
summary: Nightmare-Eclipse expands its vendetta against Microsoft with two new zero-day exploits. The researcher has published RoguePlanet and GreatXML, which are local privilege escalation exploits. This is the latest in a series of attacks targeting Windows.
insight: Zero-day exploits threaten vulnerable Windows systems with unpatched vulnerabilities.

title: Various vendors add AMD EXPO Ultra-Low Latency to 600-series motherboards in latest BIOS updates tech tightens memory subtimings on compatible kits, boosting FPS by up to 4%
summary: Various vendors release BIOS updates with AMD EXPO support for 600-series boards, tightening memory subtimings and boosting FPS by up to 4%. These updates are designed to improve gaming performance on compatible systems. The feature is part of AMD's EXPO technology.
insight: Improved gaming performance, tightened memory subtimings for AMD EXPO support.

title: Republican lawmakers urge federal agency to block imports of infringing TSMC chips as patent ruling nears five asserted U.S. patents come from United Microelectronics Corporation
summary: Four Republican lawmakers urge the ITC to block infringing chip imports.
insight: Protecting U.S. patent rights in the semiconductor industry

title: Espressif ESP32-E22 WiFi 6E module gets Wi-Fi CERTIFIED certificate, open-source WiFi and Bluetooth Linux drivers
summary: Espressif's ESP32-E22 WiFi 6E module has achieved Wi-Fi CERTIFIED status and open-source drivers.
insight: Advancements in wireless technology for host-based systems gain momentum.

title: Delicate touch: Tactile sensors help humanoids get a grip
summary: Bosch is leveraging MEMS technology for humanoids to improve tactile sensing, enabling more precise interactions. This advancement is expected to drive growth in the industry. The investment in robot startups also doubles, indicating a surge in interest.
insight: Advanced tactile sensors enhance humanoids' grip and interaction capabilities

title: ST notches market lead for in-car sensors
summary: European regulation is driving the adoption of in-car sensors to detect driver distraction and drowsiness, leading to a growth in occupant sensor technology.
insight: Safety regulations boost in-car sensor adoption across European market.

title: SpaceX goes public in the largest IPO ever, and Musk crosses the trillion-dollar line
summary: SpaceX went public in a record-breaking IPO, raising $75 billion and valuing the company at an enormous sum.
insight: SpaceX's massive valuation cements Elon Musk's status as a trillionaire.

title: Windows Server is getting new network safety capabilities with DNS over HTTPS
summary: Windows Server 2025 now supports DNS over HTTPS for encrypted DNS traffic.
insight: Enhanced network safety through secure client-to-server communications.

title: Flock AI cameras allegedly used by police to stalk ex-girlfriends and partners
summary: Police officers allegedly misuse Flock AI cameras to stalk ex-partners, sparking investigations.
insight: Abuse of surveillance tech erodes trust in law enforcement and public safety.

title: Android engineer leaves Google amid concerns over defense partnerships
summary: Ren Mayrhofer, an Android engineer, left Google citing concerns over defense partnerships.
insight: Google's shift from neutrality sparks engineer's moral and ethical concerns.

title: Chris Seedor once spent 1,500 Bitcoin on a GPU. Now he insures crypto fortunes
summary: Chris Seedor's journey began with 2011 bitcoin inheritance, initially dismissed as worthless. He later turned this fortune into a lucrative business, insuring crypto assets for others. Today, he helps people protect their digital wealth.
insight: Turning dismissed digital assets into a lucrative business opportunity

title: Arm is bringing Android its own version of DLSS game with Unreal MegaLights will show it off
summary: Arm's upcoming tech demo, Neural Dawn, showcases advanced mobile graphics capabilities, including upscaling, denoising, frame generation, and ray tracing with dynamic lights. It will be exclusive to devices with an upcoming successor to the mobile graphics chip. This tech demo aims to demonstrate the power of mobile graphics.
insight: Advancements in mobile graphics can match high-end PC experiences.

title: Samsung is building floating data centers on ships, and it's already got regulatory approval
summary: Samsung Heavy Industries is developing floating data centers with partners. This innovation aims to provide sustainable and efficient data hosting facilities. The project has already received regulatory approval.
insight: Revolutionizing data hosting with sustainable, self-sustaining AI facilities on sea

title: This jacket can harvest drinking water from the air like a real-life Dune stillsuit
summary: Engineers at the University of Texas at Austin have developed textile fibers that can collect moisture from the air and turn it into liquid water, providing a potential solution for water scarcity.
insight: Revolutionizes access to clean drinking water in drought-stricken areas globally.

title: Intel's Project Firefly is borrowing smartphone parts to build a better budget laptop
summary: Intel's Project Firefly aims to unify the entry-level PC category with a simplified laptop ecosystem.
insight: Unifying laptop ecosystem improves user experience and affordability.

title: AMD changes rules, denies researcher $10,000 bounty after taking 124 days to patch security flaw
summary: AMD denied a $10,000 bounty to a researcher after discovering a vulnerability, citing a 124-day response time, which the researcher argues is too long.
insight: Timely patching of security flaws is crucial for public trust in tech companies.
